chief_complaint: A 26-year-old male presents with new-onset seizures.
history: The patient has a history of seizures. Neurocysticercosis (NCC) is a leading cause of acquired epilepsy and other neurological disturbances worldwide. Developmental venous anomaly (DVA) is the most common cerebral vascular malformation, typically asymptomatic and discovered incidentally.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: MRI brain shows a DVA found adjacent to an NCC lesion. Serum cysticercosis antibody testing is positive.
